title: calandrinia caulescens subsp. menziesii
intro: Calandrinia caulescens subsp. menziesii, also known as Menzies' purslane, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Montiaceae. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, to treat various health issues such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Menzies' purslane include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
